Of particular note is that running this experiment for a campaign that has a very high donation intention could potentially skew the results. We suggest running this experiment again for a campaign that does not have a donor base that is as highly motivated as this one. This might provide better learning as to whether or not showing a video on a donation page either helps or hinders the number of donors that actually make a gift.
Also of note, while revenue experienced a 55.6% increase with a 99% level of confidence, this number was skewed by a couple of large individual donations.

experiments.title: How Asking Donors for a Donation Amount Based on Their Prior Giving Impacted Donor Conversion During a High Urgency Campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that asking donors for a donation amount based on their prior giving will increase donor conversion.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 05/10/2022
Live Action is a pro-life organization that uses online content and breaking-news stories to change hearts and minds on abortion and reveal abortion’s impact on everyone involved. During their 5-day Spring Monthly Donor High Urgency Campaign, Live Action wanted to see if they could increase donor conversion by asking each donor for a specific donation amount that was based on their prior giving history. Donors receiving this treatment would be thanked for their gift and told that instead of that amount, they could have an even greater impact by giving this new amount on an ongoing basis as monthly donors.
The Control version would say we needed 250 new monthly donors by midnight on April 30th. The treatment version would use a chart of standards AND the donor’s prior gift to calculate a giving amount and donor goal. Example:
Friend of Life, this is why I’m looking for 100 donors to step forward and give $10 by Saturday, April 30th. Can you be one of these donors who saves lives every single day? I know you have given as much as $10 in the past, but today I’m not asking for that much this one time. Instead, would you consider giving just 10 to start your monthly partnership? 2,300 babies face death every day.
We hypothesized that this approach that combined a chart of standards AND donors prior to giving history would increase conversion. We ensured that the customized giving amount would push to the donation page and prepopulate the donor form.
experiments.keyLearnings:
Unfortunately, due to the small sample size, we were not able to approach statistical reliability. However, we did validate this approach with the lapsed donor file during the same campaign.

People primarily give to people, not organizations or causes. By reminding the donor of their last gift and talking about how this new amount could extend the impact of their generosity further, we increased donor conversion. We will update this analysis with a breakdown of the reactivation rates by donor segment (broadbase and midlevel) to see if this approach had a greater impact on one audience over another.

We will also take every opportunity we can to give donors tangible appeals that are directly linked to their history of support and relationship with the organization.

experiments.title: How a Personal Introduction at the Beginning of an Appeal to Non-Doners Impacts Donor Conversion During a High Urgency Campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that a personal introduction at the beginning of an appeal will increase donor conversion
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/26/2022 - 04/26/2022
KCBI is a leading Christian radio station and ministry in Dallas, Texas. For their spring fundraising campaign, they wanted to see if beginning an appeal with a personal story and introduction by the sender would increase donor conversion. When testing with another organization, we found that beginning an appeal like this increased engagement by humanizing the copy and building rapport. We know from research that donors give to people, not organizations, so we wanted to see if this would increase conversion during an urgent campaign.
experiments.keyLearnings: Unfortunately, while we had a great number recipients open both versions, our donations were too few to achieve statistical reliability. We will keep testing into more conversational and personalized intro copy to see if we can validate this approach with their audience.

experiments.title: How removing a gift array from a donation page impacts revenue during a high urgency campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that removing the gift array from the campaign donation page for page visitors  will achieve increased conversions and higher revenue per visitor.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/16/2021 - 07/09/2021
RTB’s donation pages always have a gift array to offer a preselected giving amount for the site visitor. For RTB’s 2021 FYE campaign, we wanted to test removing the gift array on the campaign donation page to see how this would impact giving (donations and revenue). The idea behind it is that visitors would give more without being guided by a gift array and having clear direction to choose their own giving amount.
experiments.keyLearnings:
While revenue only decreased 6.7% with a level of confidence of 55%, donor conversion rate had a directional decrease of 11.9% (LoC 88%). Though the data is not statistically valid, the results seem to indicate the the gift array is helpful in providing people a guide on how much they should give. If they aren't given pre-selected amounts, they are less likely to give on the donation page. It is recommended that further testing is done on the gift array. Changing the amounts, the ascending/descending order and the placement in relation to the one-time/monthly option are all potential next steps for testing.
Observations of the impact on other visitor segments in the experiment:
Email visitors had a 101.6% increase in revenue with a 94% level of confidence.Mobile visitors had a 139.4% increase in revenue with an 89% level of confidence.New  visitors had a 131.7% increase in revenue with a 92% level of confidence.

experiments.title: How adding a thermometer and value proposition language impacts donor conversion in a higher urgency campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding clarity through value proposition language and motivation through a giving thermometer for potential monthly donors will achieve increased monthly donor conversions.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 09/24/2021 - 10/01/2021
RTB was running their monthly partner campaign in which their goal was to grow their number of monthly donors. We ran a test on RTB’s monthly partner donation page where two variables were tested.
We added value proposition language to each monthly giving button/option. The thought was that we could increase donor conversion rate by providing clarity on what their gift is going towards.
We also added a thermometer that indicated new monthly donors compared to RTB’s monthly partner campaign goal. We thought that adding clarity through value prop to what different levels of monthly giving can provide in addition to a visible goal that visitors can contribute to would lead to more monthly donor conversions.
experiments.keyLearnings: The treatment resulted in fewer conversions than the control; however, the difference was marginal.  This experiment resulted in a 68% decrease in donor conversion with a level of confidence of 70%. Therefore the results of this experiment did not validate. The run time of the experiment was only a week and could have validated given more time. However, there were two vastly different concepts/variables tested in this experiment. It is recommended that this is retested by isolating a single variable. From this experiment, we don't know if the fewer conversions were a result of the thermometer, added value prop, or both.

experiments.title: How adding a humanized dear reader to blogs during a high urgency campaign affects donor conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a humanized dear reader during calendar year end for blog readers will achieve a higher donor conversion rate .
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/22/2021 - 01/06/2022
During calendar year end, Avila wanted to switch out their typical offer-driven dear reader with a direct appeal for the calendar year end campaign. However, we wanted to humanize this dear reader by adding the founder’s (Dan Burke) picture and signature so that the message and ask were much more humanized. The control of this experiment is written from the perspective of the organization, while the treatment is written from the prospective of the founder. These dear readers were placed at the bottom of the blogs and were live during the last couple weeks of the campaign.
All traffic to these pages were organic.
experiments.keyLearnings: The results of this experiment did not validate and did not indicate any kind of directional lift. The run time of this experiment was fairly short and did not reach the necessary sample size. It's recommended that this concept is tested again while using an offer in the dear reader. This would allow for more time for the experiment to validate and would give us more data on how their audience responds to hearing a message from the founder himself.

experiments.title: How adding urgency copy w/ a specific ask affects donations
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that asking for a specific $ amount and specific number of donors for People visiting the website will achieve A higher number of donations than a less specific ask..
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 03/30/2022 - 04/04/2022
I Am Second wanted to see if changing the copy to have a sense of urgency with a specific ask would increase donations when added to a pop up. For the high urgency fiscal year end (FYE) campaign, IAS ran an experiment with 2 versions of a sticky bar pop up that asked for support.
In the control version, the copy asked if the potential donor would make a donation by Midnight on March 31st (IAS FYE). In the treatment version, there was specific copy that asked for “10 people to give $50 by midnight”.
The goal was to see if using a smaller believable amount of people and $ amount needed by a specific time would lead to more donations.
experiments.keyLearnings:
While the experiment did not reach statistical validity due to not enough sample size, we did have some positive learnings. We saw a 106.7% increase in donations overall during this very short timeframe. While we saw a 77% Level of Confidence in these results, we did not have a large enough sample size to draw any concrete resolutions. However, we saw enough directional lift to say that this tactic would be worth experimenting with again on future campaigns.
Changes to future experiments may include a phased approach to urgency, where we start with a more generic ask similar to the control, then switch to a version similar to the treatment version with more specificity closer to the close of a high urgency campaign…to have more credibility. After-all…urgency cannot be sped up, only highlighted.
Notable changes to other metrics due to the experiment:
Revenue experienced a 275.3% increase with a 96% level of confidence.
Observations of the impact on other visitor segments in the experiment:
Organic Search visitors had a 2.0% increase in donations with a 92% level of confidence.

experiments.title: How a text message impacts campaign giving in a high urgency campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that sending a text message during a high urgency campaign will increase downstream donations among their housefile.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 03/18/2022
During a high urgency campaign TFAS wanted to test using text messaging for the end of their high urgency campaign, Founders Day. Typically, TFAS uses slybroadcast in the final days of their campaign to remind their constituents to give during the campaign. We wanted to determine how effective testing would be by splitting the mobile users list in half. Half would receive the text and half would not. We then looked at downstream conversions during the campaign to see if there was an increase in giving among those who received the text
experiments.keyLearnings: The results of this experiment did not validate. There is no statistical difference between those who did or did not receive the text message in the campaign. This means that this text was most likely not a motivator for those to take action during the campaign. Another potential test is to use text messaging as a way to engage users (not just an ask) and see if this type of engagement produces more donations during the campaign.

experiments.title: How adding communal language to a sticky bar during a high urgency campaign affects donor conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding communal language to a sticky bar for web visitors will achieve an increase in donor conversion rate.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 02/18/2022 - 03/04/2022
The Fund for American Studies was running a high urgency campaign in February called Founders Day. They ran site-wide sticky bars in order to drive more donations for their campaign. We wanted to test using communal language to inspire more people to give to this campaign. We used the phrase “Because you’re a friend to TFAS” in order to communicate that to the web visitor that they are part of the organization’s work.
All traffic for this campaign was organic web traffic.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The results of this experiment did not validate most likely due to the fact that this experiment needed more time to run. It is recommended that this test is ran again. One thing to note is that we did see a directional lift in donor conversion among email visitors. This is most likely due to the fact that people on their file would resonate with being referred to as a "friend of TFAS."

Notable changes to other metrics due to the experiment:
Revenue experienced a 88.3% decrease with a 86% level of confidence.
Email visitors had a 353.8% increase in donations with an 89% level of confidence.
 	Desktop visitors had a 196.3% increase in donations with a 92% level of confidence.

experiments.title: How emailing for a well-known sender in a high urgency campaign impacted email engagement from the housefile
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that emails from a well-known sender during a high urgency campaign for housefile subscribers will achieve an increase in opens and clicks.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 04/05/2021
During a high urgency campaign, we wanted to test multiple senders to assess how the housefile responded to emails from different team members. Because they receive a weekly cultivation email from the Vice President of Development, we thought we might see higher engagement on emails sent from him during the campaign rather than a member of the Membership Programs team since they are familiar with hearing from him regularly.
experiments.keyLearnings: We did not have a large enough sample size to validate the test. We saw a slight decrease in the open rate on the treatment email but only with 46% level of confidence. Going forward, we will want to also measure the click rate and donation conversion rate for each sender to see if there is any significant difference in response.

experiments.title: How a problem-specific subject line impacted email engagement in a high urgency campaign

rows.name: Treatment - The Left is relentless. So are you.

experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding mystery to the subject line for the housefile in a high urgency campaign will achieve an increase in the open rate.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 04/05/2021
During a high urgency campaign, most of the subject lines were in reference to membership and were relevant to the campaign. We wanted to find out if a problem-oriented subject line that held some mystery about the contents of the message would increase open and click rates for the email. We split the file 50/50 and send the control subject line “About your membership” vs. “The Left is relentless. So are you.” both from the same sender.
experiments.keyLearnings: We did not validate the experiment since we only reached a 16% level of confidence that there was a .24% increase in the open rate on the treatment. Since there was no significant change observed, we will have to test this concept again on future emails or try other methods to improve the email engagement rate during a high urgency campaign with frequent emails. We want to find out if the housefile is more responsive to membership-specific messages concerning their status or if they are more intrigued by the problem we are facing and need their help to solve.

experiments.title: How an urgency-focused sticky bar on an email acquisition page affects conversion rates
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding an urgency-focused sticky bar for landing page visitors will achieve greater ebook downloads.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/07/2020 - 12/15/2020
In this experiment, we wondered if adding a sticky bar callout on a new ebook’s download page could create a heightened sense of urgency and lead to more conversions on the page.
The book was focused on strategies to help grow fundraising results during the last week of the year. So the sticky bar emphasized that the last week of the year is coming soon.
experiments.keyLearnings:
We saw no significant difference between the two variants. The sticky bar had a small decrease of 4.8% but at a low confidence level (67%).

Now, we have seen sticky bars like this perform well in other areas. Likely, the specific emphasis of the copy didn't create the intended level of urgency - so it had nearly no effect on conversion.

It would be worth re-testing the sticky bar with new messaging or even a countdown clock to try and invoke greater urgency and see if that can lead to a significant result.

experiments.title: How running a brief cultivation series impacted the amount NIO ticket revenue by a sale deadline
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that sending cultivation emails for half of our NIO housefile will achieve an increase of NIO Summit ticket revenue by the end of the early bird deadline.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 05/07/2021 - 06/13/2021
Approaching the NIO Summit Early Bird ticket deadline, we wanted to test whether cultivating half of our file would increase the number of tickets sold by the end of the deadline.
We split our housefile in half and sent our regular marketing emails and NIO emails to both lists, while sending 4 bonus emails to the treatment group. These emails included various NIO resources like past sessions and blog posts. The thinking was that sending these resources will show the value of NIO Summit and increase excitement for the event.
This is the calculation of the total revenue brought in by each segment. These results were calculated by removing all test transactions and free tickets sold.
experiments.keyLearnings: Because of the low percentage in confidence in these results, this hypothesis requires more testing.

experiments.title: How strengthening the value proposition of the wiki banner impacts donor conversion during a high urgency campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: Can increasing the clarity of the value proposition on the wiki banner increase donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/13/2021 - 07/15/2021
Focus on the Family uses a banner at the top of their website during high urgency campaigns to capture site traffic and convert them into donors. The messaging for this banner is always in alignment with the campaign messaging. It usually includes a headline and a direct call to action. We hypothesized that we could increase donor conversion by increasing the clarity of the value proposition in the banner. To do this, we developed a treatment that communicated the problem and presented a call-to-action as the solution.
experiments.keyLearnings:
For all traffic, there was no positive or negative impact on donor conversion. That said, this neutral result is due to the significant differences in impact with opposing segments. Specifically, when looking at new visitors to the site, there was a 4% decrease in conversion; while with returning visitors, there was a 5% increase in donor conversion. What we can learn from this experiment is that the motivation of users on the site are wildly different and the additional value proposition is needed for some site visitors, but not all. This can be dependent upon other messaging they have seen in other channels, and even people's familiarity with the organization. One specific trend we saw in the data is that when people were coming from an outside channel where they may have read or seen messaging for the campaign, the increased value proposition helped to increase donor conversion when people got to the page because the message they had previously read was reinforced through the wiki banner on the top of the website.
Other observations of the impact on visitor segments in the experiment:
Direct visitors had a 14.7% decrease in donations with a 100% level of confidence.Email visitors had a 16.3% increase in donations with a 100% level of confidence.Paid Search visitors had a 47.3% increase in donations with a 100% level of confidence.Social visitors had a 74.8% increase in donations with a 100% level of confidence.Female visitors had a 35.7% increase in donations with a 100% level of confidence.Male visitors had a 36.0% increase in donations with a 100% level of confidence.New  visitors had a 4.1% decrease in donations with a 93% level of confidence.Returning  visitors had a 4.7% increase in donations with a 97% level of confidence.

experiments.title: How a subject line test impacted open rates on a high urgency campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that creating a new subject line for returning donors will achieve an increase in open rate.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 04/01/2021
In an effort to increase overall email engagement during a two-week high urgency annual campaign, we wanted to test various subject lines to measure open and click response. We developed a treatment that used more active rather than passive language. The control subject line asked the question “Are you ready?” while the treatment asked “Are you coming?” while the body copy remained the same for both versions.
experiments.keyLearnings: We did not see any valid results from the experiment and only reached 50.2% level of confidence so this concept would need to be re-tested in the future. Looking at the body copy for the treatment, we would want to also test aligning the body copy with the subject line to make sure the original question is addressed or expounded upon to help the message flow more congruently.

experiments.title: How adding a recurring donor ticker affected a high urgency campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: Will including the live total number of donors acquired towards the overall goal increase conversion rate on a donation page?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 09/15/2020 - 09/24/2020
With a specific goal set for a high urgency campaign, we wanted to test if adding a ticker to the donation page in a sticky bar to count the number of monthly donors would increase donor conversion.
experiments.keyLearnings: While we did not reach the minimum level of confidence needed for validity of the experiment and saw a slight decrease in the number of transactions, we did notice that the average gift increased on the treatment, which is something we would want to test further to evaluate. We also hypothesized that the results could have been negatively influenced since the campaign met its monthly donor goal ahead of schedule, there may have been a reduced sense of need or urgency when visitors arrived on the page and saw the goal had already been met before they decided to contribute. For future tests, we want to launch the sticky bar earlier in the campaign to see how it affects motivation and we also want to test replacing the text-only version sticky bar with additional visuals like a progress bar or thermometer to further add clarity to the progress of the goal and what is being counted.

experiments.title: How the addition of a dynamic sticky bar during a high urgency campaign impacts donor conversion
experiments.researchQuestion: Can we increase donor motivation with the addition of a sticky bar on the donation page?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/09/2019 - 01/02/2020
During Care Net’s year-end campaign, they wanted to see if they could increase the number of donations from their primary donation page by including a sticky bar at the top of the page that reminded people how many donations they needed to reach their goal. The sticky bar was dynamic and would count down as donations were being made.
experiments.keyLearnings: The sticky bar had no difference on donor conversion. These results were slightly surprising as we have seen this tactic help improve donor conversion for other organizations. That said, this experiment was performed during the highest giving season and donor motivation in a year. Our hypothesis is that we didn’t see a difference in conversion between the control and the treatment because donor motivation was already high. We’d like to test this same tactic when motivation is more neutral to see if we can create urgency during normal giving seasons.

experiments.title: How visually reinforcing a deadline impacts store conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: Will visually reinforcing the December 25th deadline increase the conversion rate of the site?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/22/2016 - 12/25/2016
One of the most successful fundraising campaigns for World Concern is their Global Gift Guide, especially leading up to Christmas. There is a high volume of traffic going to the site in the week prior to December 25th. With that as a natural deadline, we wanted to see if we could visually increase the urgency of the Christmas holiday.
We created an animated drop down that would appear when visitors first arrived at the site. It contained language that reinforced the overall value proposition of the organization and an animated countdown clock that would remind them of the Christmas deadline. There was no call to action since the Gift Guide has many options for people to choose.
experiments.keyLearnings: With a limited time span available leading up to Christmas, we were not able to validate any significant lift. However, it would appear the drop down may have increased conversion rate if there had been more time to collect traffic. In future iterations of this concept, it is recommended that we find a specific item or gift to highlight to give the dropdown a specific call to action instead of the more general value proposition.

experiments.title: How the addition of a goal affects donor conversion rate in a high-urgency campaign
experiments.researchQuestion: Does the mention of a campaign goal in a high urgency campaign motivate more people to give?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 07/03/2018
The Missionary Oblates were running their fiscal year end high urgency campaign and wanted to test whether the inclusion of a campaign goal motivated more people to click through to the donation page and give. They ran this on each email of the campaign , with copy in the emails being largely the same other than the inclusion or exclusion of the goal amount.
experiments.keyLearnings: The experiment was not statistically valid, but we saw a directional decrease of 11% in donations with the removal of the campaign goal. This shows us that it’s likely more people will give towards a goal rather than an open-ended ask.

experiments.title: How year-end messaging and urgency indicators affect donation page revenue
experiments.researchQuestion: How will specific year-end messaging and urgency indicators affect donation page revenue?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/21/2017 - 01/03/2018
Compassion International wanted to run a test on their main donation page at the end of year. Typically, people came to this page to make year-end gifts, with traffic and revenue ramping up sharply in December. However, the copy on the page remained the same all year, with nothing special done to reflect the year-end deadline.
They wanted to test copy and urgency indicators to see if they could positively affect motivation and generate more revenue from the page during the last half of December.
They crafted a treatment page with a new headline: “Make your year end donation today”. They added copy that referenced the December 31st deadline, added a story to show the impact of the donation, and added a countdown clock to visually show urgency in the sidebar.
They launched an A/B test to determine a winner.
experiments.keyLearnings: The year-end treatment page showed a directional lift in revenue (14.1% increase), but it was unable to achieve statistical validity. This outlines the difficulty of testing at a time of the year where there is already high motivation. The treatment page might produce a lift, but because conversion is higher than normal on the control, there wasn’t enough separation to determine a winner.

experiments.title: How urgency on a donation page affects donor conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does increasing urgency on a donation page increase donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 09/01/2017 - 09/29/2017
Buckner was testing more urgent copy for an immediate need on the donation page of their content offer, 7 Scriptures to Pray for the Child in your Life. The immediate need focused around Hurricane Harvey and the Buckner staff and families affected by the disaster. The ads and acquisition page for the offer remained the same.
experiments.keyLearnings: This test did not reach 95% validation or reach statistical power of over 80%, so the test results were not valid. However, we did see a trend towards a higher gift amount on the treatment page, likely due to the urgency of the need. It is also likely that the time window for donations to Harvey had passed once the treatment began, which could have had some effect on conversion rate.

experiments.title: How emphasizing the urgency of a campaign impacts donor conversion
experiments.researchQuestion: Will donation page copy that emphasizes the relevance and urgency of Jack’s case increase donor acquisition?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 10/17/2017 - 11/30/2017
Alliance Defending Freedom was looking for new ways to optimize the performance of their Jack Phillips donation page. They wondered: Can we positively impact visitor motivation and by increasing the relevance and urgency donating to support the Jack Phillip’s case? They created a treatment version and launched an A/B test to find out.
experiments.keyLearnings: Alliance Defending Freedom found that this test produced no statistically significant results. Therefore, the impact of the treatment version is inconclusive. Still, the fact that this page did not produce a valid result does tell us that the copy used on the treatment page to emphasize the urgency of this case did not increase visitor motivation.

experiments.title: How the placement of elements to add urgency affects conversion
experiments.researchQuestion: Which placement of the countdown clock and thermometer of the donation goal will have the greatest impact on donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/03/2015 - 06/04/2015
Previous experiments had taught us the positive impact increasing urgency and progress toward a goal can have an a campaign. As part of a moneybomb campaign, The Heritage Foundation wanted to determine if the placement of the visual elements designed to promote these two factors would impact the donor conversion.
For the campaign, we designed two landing pages: one with a thermometer at the top and a countdown clock at the bottom and another with these two visual elements flipped.
experiments.keyLearnings: Regardless of whether the countdown clock or thermometer was displayed at the top of the page, the pages had nearly identical conversion rate. From past experiments, we know both of these elements will boost campaign performance. What we learned here is that their placement is less important than simply being included in the page.
